text.title: Cache Hit Audit Script Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Audit reports total cache ratio. Looks fine at 75% even when a single prefix dropped from 92% to 30%.
antipattern.fix: Cluster by prefix_hash and surface per-cluster ratios.

antipattern.symptom: Each day's number reported absolute. Ops can't tell whether 65% is good or bad.
antipattern.root-cause: No 7-day rolling average + no per-prefix prev-day delta.
antipattern.fix: Compute delta_vs_7d at aggregate level and ratio_delta_pp at prefix level.

antipattern.symptom: System prompt interpolates `{{user_name}}` per call. Cache ratio drops to ~10%.
antipattern.root-cause: Author thought caching would handle small variations; in practice the prefix must be byte-identical.
antipattern.fix: Move all per-user vars to user message; system prompt is byte-stable across users.

antipattern.symptom: Every minor variance triggers Slack alert. Team mutes the channel; real regressions get missed.
antipattern.root-cause: Threshold too low (5pp); window too short (1h).
antipattern.fix: Threshold 20pp delta + min 100 calls/day. Aggregate alert 15pp over 24h vs 7d.
